forecast-period.text: A high pressure system south of the state will move to the southeast of Tasmania today, but will maintain a ridge over SA ahead of a cold front during Tuesday and Wednesday. Another high will move across waters to the south later Wednesday and will be centred southeast of Tasmania late Thursday morning.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Morning fog patches in the south. Dry apart from isolated morning showers about southern coasts and ranges. Maximum temperatures above average in the west, but near or slightly below average in the east. Southeasterly winds tending northeasterly in the west.

Morning fog patches in the south. Dry apart from isolated showers and a possible thunderstorm in the far southwest late afternoon and evening. Maximum temperatures above average in the west and south, but near average in the northeast. Northeast to northwesterly winds, fresh at times in the west, shifting southwest to southeasterly in the far southwest. East to southeasterly winds in the far northeast.

Isolated light showers about southern coastal districts, mostly during the morning and early afternoon. Maximum temperatures above average. Northeast to northwesterly winds, but east to southeasterly in the far northeast, ahead of a south to southeasterly change extending across the south.

text.p: Dry. Morning fog patches about eastern districts. Maximum temperatures above average. Northeast to northerly winds, but east to southeasterly in the far northeast.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Wednesday: up to 2 mm for the agricultural area and West Coast district, locally reaching 2 to 5 mm near southern coasts and ranges and the South East districts. Isolated falls of 5 to 10 mm are possible about the Lower South East.
